shorts.title: Stop Learning Python Wrong (Setup Matters First)
shorts.description: Most tutorials teach syntax before thinking. That's backwards. Setup your environment first—then everything clicks. Tutorial 1/10. #Shorts #Python #DataScience #Coding #Learning
shorts.hook_visual: Large on-screen text: 'You're learning Python wrong.' Cut to confused developer staring at error. Then cut to clean terminal showing 'source env/bin/activate' with checkmarks appearing.
shorts.end_screen_cta: Watch the full tutorial to build your foundation correctly. Link in bio.

shorts.title: Two Ways People Learn Python
shorts.description: Most learners collect syntax rules like trading cards — and drown by week six. Data science demands the second path: learning to think about problems first, then using Python to express solutions. You're not learning a language. You're learning to communicate with data. #Python #DataScience #Shorts

shorts.title: Wrong Setup Kills Your DS Projects
shorts.description: Most tutorials skip the part that actually matters: environment setup. Bad isolation means your code runs today, breaks in six months, and you can't trace why. Here's what a real data science environment looks like. #DataScience #Python #Shorts

shorts.title: Activate This Before Every Python Session
shorts.description: One line keeps your entire Python environment clean — source env/bin/activate. Run it every single time you sit down to code, or you risk version conflicts and messy global installs. This habit alone saves hours of debugging. #Python #DataScience #Shorts

shorts.title: Why Your Code Breaks in Docker
shorts.description: One project worked on my laptop, completely failed in a container — same code, different environment. I didn't lose time because Python was hard. I lost time because the setup was sloppy. Treat environments like engineering, not cleanup. #DataScience #Python #Shorts

shorts.title: Think Like a Programmer Before You Code
shorts.description: Running code that works and understanding why it works are two very different things. Computational thinking — decomposition, pattern recognition, abstraction — is the mental model that separates frustrated beginners from confident programmers. Learn to think first, code second. #Python #DataScience #Shorts

shorts.title: Reading Python Errors: The Skill That Compounds
shorts.description: Most tutorials skip this — but reading error messages is what separates people who learn Python from people who quit. A KeyError tells you exactly what went wrong and where. This is the skill that actually compounds. #Python #Shorts #DataScience

shorts.title: The First Skill Python Tutorials Skip
shorts.description: Before you write a single line, break the problem into pieces. Decomposition — not syntax — is what separates someone who 'learned Python' from someone who can actually use it. This is where data science thinking starts. #Shorts #Python #DataScience

shorts.title: You'll Write This Pattern 1,000 Times in Data Science
shorts.description: Load data. Loop through it. Accumulate a result. Store the output. That's it. You'll see this structure in simple scripts and in production pipelines with millions of rows. Learn it small. It scales. #Shorts #Python #DataScience

shorts.title: Skip Hello World. Write This Instead.
shorts.description: Your first Python program should touch structured data, not strings. A list of dicts, a loop, a saved result. That's real data science thinking — and it already reflects the structure behind production systems. #Shorts #Python #DataScience

shorts.title: One Line That Will Save Your Data Science Projects
shorts.description: `source env/bin/activate` — run it every single time you sit down to code. Clean environment. No version conflicts. The most important line in your Python journey isn't in your script. It's the one you run before you open it. #Shorts #Python #DataScience

shorts.title: Your Code Ran. But Can You Recreate It?
shorts.description: The code worked. You got the answer. Six months later, nothing runs. Bad environment setup is why most first data science projects fail silently — not because Python is hard, but because reproducibility was never built in. #Shorts #Python #DataScience

shorts.title: Python Errors Are Feedback. Read Them Like This.
shorts.description: KeyError, TypeError, IndexError — Python tells you exactly what went wrong and where. The people who quit think 'I'm not smart enough.' The people who make it treat every error as a debugging instruction. Run, read, fix, run again. #Shorts #Python #DataScience

shorts.title: Why Simple Python Scripts Scale Further Than You Think
shorts.description: Input data. Transform it. Save the output. That structure works for a 10-row CSV and for a pipeline processing millions of records in a warehouse. The foundations don't change — the scale does. Learn them right the first time. #Shorts #Python #DataScience
